definition: A methodological innovation that separates confidence in the direction/sign of a demographic effect from confidence in its magnitude, avoiding the systematic misleading that occurs when collapsing into single confidence numbers. Applied throughout the synthesis: 9/10 direction but 5/10 magnitude on fiscal deterioration, 7/10 sign but 4/10 magnitude on aging-r*.
evidence: Pair_2 identified this as 'genuinely useful and should become standard practice'
Applied to fiscal deterioration (9/10 direction, 5/10 magnitude)
Applied to China (9/10 demographics, 4/10 financial translation)
open_questions: Should this methodology be formalized into all KB entries?
How should split confidence interact with portfolio sizing decisions?
Can the methodology be extended to timing uncertainty?
